programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Caroline and Gary fulfil their dream of owning a house in Spain for regular getaways to the countryside and villages of Valencia province. Jasmine shows them five fantastic options for their budget of £150,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karen and David look for a perfect place in Paphos for £360k. Will a stunning stone villa that has David moving in immediately or a place with splendid sea views and a swanky pool deliver?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second to host in the Roman Spa town of Bath is ex-raver and single mum Mel Lewis-Leger. Mels treating her guests to a night of Cajun food and an `old school rave party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day three of the competition in Bath and the turn of handyman Jon Wicheard to wow his four guests. Jon is a man of simple, yet strange taste, bemusing his fellow diners by dousing all his meals in his special sauce: tomato ketchup.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fourth to host in Bath is cafe worker Becky Tyler. With her day job heavily influencing her choice of starter, Becky sets out to put on a menu of great British fare.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition in Bath and its the turn of patriotic Frenchman Denis Verrier. Working in the hospitality industry, Denis is pretty confident he knows what it takes to throw an amazing dinner party.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

Previously both couples were in a race against time to find family homes and move in before the women gave birth. programme.title: Five Card Stud
programme.desc: After a cardsharp is caught cheating, he is taken out and lynched by the drunkards he was playing against. Soon afterwards, the men who were in the lynch mob start being murdered, one after another; all by hanging. Who will be killed next and who is responsible? Is it one of the original party seeking to cover their accursed deed, or perhaps the mysterious Rev Jonathan Rudd, who has recently arrived in town?

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete and NTS build a five-platform nature headquarters for the For-Mar Nature Preserve in Michigan, helping a community still reeling from the 2014 water crisis.

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: Meet the lizard obsessive who changed his name to Mr Lizard Lover and found fame taking photos of his scaly friends posing as humans.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations explain the bizarre, the brilliant and the most mind-blowing animal behaviours.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Why do dogs bury bones? Can elephants detect bombs? Why do birds sing in the morning? Plus, what makes seahorses almost indestructible?

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: On one of his most audacious and exciting expeditions to date, Forrest travels to the remote Galapagos Islands in search of the fabled Fernandina tortoise.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People: Bushcraft Chronicles
programme.desc: Featuring exclusive interviews and insider facts, these are Browntowns best hunts and food sources. Bird and Gabe make a bush fertiliser from Amis home recipe.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: MMA fighter Urijah Faber just opened his new gym and wants a bit of the ocean inside. Plus, a couple wants a tank for the Marine Aquarium Convention.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The fall cattle round up takes an electrifying turn when a horse gets tangled in electric wire. Plus, Atz Sr takes Bonnie on an epic hunting date. Then, Eivin and Levi go on a rock-climbing expedition.

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy and his sons search the frozen wilderness for a lost bulldozer. Mark sets out to show his new fiancee the beauty of McCarthy, but finds danger.

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: When Jeremy crosses a frozen river in a two-ton bulldozer, he finds himself on thin ice. Jenny takes on a dangerous survival mission with her new-born baby.

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: After PJ moves onto a plantation, her health deteriorates and her animals fall sick. A local Medicine Man tries to lift the curse on this Native American battleground.

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: On one of his most audacious and exciting expeditions to date, Forrest travels to the remote Galapagos Islands in search of the fabled Fernandina tortoise.

programme.title: Haisiyat
programme.desc: In a Bombay mill the industrious young union leader, Ram, falls in love with the new owner, Sita, and marries her. Ram has been a diligent union boss, working successfully to improve conditions for the workers. After his marriage to the owner, conflicts begin to arise between management and the workforce, and Ram is forced to choose between his love and his honour.

programme.title: Dharmyudh
programme.desc: A young man never forgets his teacher, even after reaching a high position. Now he must come to his masters rescue.

programme.title: Khel Khiladi Ka
programme.desc: After seeing his father killed, his sister sexually assaulted and commit suicide and his brother thrown into a river by three different men, Ajit swears revenge.

programme.title: Naya Kadam
programme.desc: Madhukar and Jaggu are Hitler-like tyrants who rule over a small community with an iron hand. They prefer to keep the inhabitants illiterate, so that they can continue to oppress and abuse them over time.

programme.title: Pyar Ke Kabil
programme.desc: A young couple split soon after their marriage, splitting up the product of their marriage, twins. Many years later the twins meet again and decide to switch places to try and bring their parents together again.

programme.title: Zakhmi Aurat
programme.desc: A raped cop abandons her uniform to get revenge against her attackers by devastating means.

programme.title: Falak

programme.title: Kaali Topi Laal Rumaal
programme.desc: Vijay leads a poor life, along with his widowed mother and his brother, Raja. His mother dies under certain circumstances, after which Vijay joins the Indian Army. He becomes a commando in no time. He single-handedly arrests a terrorist.

programme.desc: Mitch and his lifeguard team rush to save Hobie, ex-wife Gayle and her fiance after their plane crashes in the water.

programme.desc: Hostage Mitch performs surgery on an ex-con; an environmental investigator has a near-fatal accident.

programme.desc: Mitch agonizes over the near-drowning of a boy who lies in a coma.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Kites and Koras on the Cosmopolitan Coast
programme.desc: Its a cosmopolitan end to dapper duo Izzie Balmer and Mark Hills trip as they sweep along the Sussex coast in search of treasures. Izzie is dazzled by a vast haul of Chinese kites, while Mark hopes that an out-of-fashion etching could be an auction sleeper. They pause their shopping in vibrant Brighton to help make a kora, the traditional instrument of west African storytellers, before hearing a maestro perform. But with the final auction looming, who will hit all the right notes?

Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guest panellists David Morrissey, Holly Willoughby, John Kearns and Montell Douglas.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Silence is golden as the queens tackle an acting challenge with a difference. Split into two teams, the queens must let their bodies do the talking as they star in silent movies, with direction from Doctor Who supremo Russell T Davies. Acting superstar Will Poulter joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Alan Carr. May the best drag queen win!

programme.title: Green Book
programme.desc: Dr Don Shirley is a world-class African-American pianist, who is about to embark on a concert tour in the Deep South in 1962. In need of a driver and protection, Shirley recruits Tony Lip, a tough-talking bouncer from an Italian-American neighbourhood in the Bronx. Despite their differences, the two men soon develop an unexpected bond while confronting racism and danger in an era of segregation.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Three generations of one family want to move to the East Midlands, and the task of finding a new home for them all has fallen on mum, grandma, and Ginny Buckley. Away from looking at houses, Ginny meets a volunteer group helping to restore the areas local canals and their footpaths so that wildlife can thrive and the public can enjoy a safer stroll.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Mark Wright, Asher Edwards and Maxine Dwyer come to the rescue of Fatma, a grandmother struggling with arthritis.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Kites and Koras on the Cosmopolitan Coast
programme.desc: Its a cosmopolitan end to dapper duo Izzie Balmer and Mark Hills trip as they sweep along the Sussex coast in search of treasures. Izzie is dazzled by a vast haul of Chinese kites, while Mark hopes that an out-of-fashion etching could be an auction sleeper. They pause their shopping in vibrant Brighton to help make a kora, the traditional instrument of west African storytellers, before hearing a maestro perform. But with the final auction looming, who will hit all the right notes?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Kites and Koras on the Cosmopolitan Coast
programme.desc: Its a cosmopolitan end to dapper duo Izzie Balmer and Mark Hills trip as they sweep along the Sussex coast in search of treasures. Izzie is dazzled by a vast haul of Chinese kites, while Mark hopes that an out-of-fashion etching could be an auction sleeper. They pause their shopping in vibrant Brighton to help make a kora, the traditional instrument of west African storytellers, before hearing a maestro perform. But with the final auction looming, who will hit all the right notes?

Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Silence is golden as the queens tackle an acting challenge with a difference. Split into two teams, the queens must let their bodies do the talking as they star in silent movies, with direction from Doctor Who supremo Russell T Davies. Acting superstar Will Poulter joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Alan Carr. May the best drag queen win!

programme.title: Green Book
programme.desc: Dr Don Shirley is a world-class African-American pianist, who is about to embark on a concert tour in the Deep South in 1962. In need of a driver and protection, Shirley recruits Tony Lip, a tough-talking bouncer from an Italian-American neighbourhood in the Bronx. Despite their differences, the two men soon develop an unexpected bond while confronting racism and danger in an era of segregation.

programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan takes an epic journey across the Gobi Desert on one of the worlds most iconic, yet least understood, animals - camels. Starting in the west of Mongolia, Gordeon crosses blistering sand dunes and endless rocky plains.

programme.title: Scotlands Big Night Out
programme.desc: On the biggest night out of the year, Iain Stirling, stand up comedian, presenter of `Dog Ate My Homework and the voice of `Love Island, explores the history of Saturday nights out across Scotland since the 1950s. He hears from those who were there at the Fountainbridge Palais, Edinburgh, the Locarno in Glasgow and Robbies in Dundee at their wildest. He speaks to the man who brought The Beatles to Bridge of Allan, and he enjoys archive footage of some of Scotlands most enjoyable nights out.

programme.title: Top Gear

Plus, James meets American stunt driving legend Ken Block, and Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive learns about his family story on an emotional visit to a former slave fort, before getting a big celebratory welcome (and a new name) from the elders of the Fante people.

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: In Lagos, one of Africas largest cities, Clive gets a workout in the downtown street market, heads for the studio with local musicians and experiences the vibrancy of Nigerian art.

programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson has a conversation with, not an interrogation of, the people who shape our political thinking about what shaped theirs.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: The dealers are in LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue as they search for rare items. While Ian picks up a pair of colourful majolica planters, Harry strikes a deal on a mysterious wooden figure and a pair of puppets.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallacks mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the villages mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: In the first of two episodes in the rich tropical waters of the Indian Ocean, the team explores its remote and pristine underwater worlds. The team investigates how schools of manta ray suffering from shark bites are treated by the inhabitants of a remarkable reef, and they go in search of one of the Indian Oceans most elusive creatures, the dugong.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The teams are at the Old College, a part of Aberystwyth University on the West Coast of Wales. A grade one listed building and the first University in Wales. Our experts Christina Trevanion and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Christina values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj. Paul Martin visits a Victorian tourist attraction that paved the way for modern photography.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Michelle Ackerley, Katie Derham, Karen Hauer and Geoff Norcott test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Redemption Week.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The week reaches its finale as Vernon Kay hosts the last stop on this weeks UK-wide adventure. TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz have one final shot at glory, with Paul Gorton leading an unreliable tour of Coleraine.

programme.title: The Wild Gardener
programme.desc: Colin digs ponds, clears brambles, plants woodland and sews meadows as he tries to figure out how best to lure the wild creatures back. Along the way, Colin travels across Britain and Ireland to meet other enthusiasts on their own wild crusades.

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty looks back at some of the remarkable gardeners shining a light on how people can grow their own nutritious food fresh from the garden.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The team returns to Okavango Delta to catch up with old friends, lionesses hunt nonstop to feed growing cubs, leopardess Xudum moves on, and the new mum, Lediba, steps into the spotlight. Pobe the cheetah returns with two cubs of her own.

programme.desc: A firefighter is on hand to try to save a Somerset terrace. Owain is taken by the period features in a Worcestershire house. And we catch up with a plot of land in Staffordshire first seen in 2022.

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive learns about his family story on an emotional visit to a former slave fort, before getting a big celebratory welcome (and a new name) from the elders of the Fante people.

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: In Lagos, one of Africas largest cities, Clive gets a workout in the downtown street market, heads for the studio with local musicians and experiences the vibrancy of Nigerian art.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson has a conversation with, not an interrogation of, the people who shape our political thinking about what shaped theirs.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: The dealers are in LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue as they search for rare items. While Ian picks up a pair of colourful majolica planters, Harry strikes a deal on a mysterious wooden figure and a pair of puppets.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallacks mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the villages mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: In the first of two episodes in the rich tropical waters of the Indian Ocean, the team explores its remote and pristine underwater worlds. The team investigates how schools of manta ray suffering from shark bites are treated by the inhabitants of a remarkable reef, and they go in search of one of the Indian Oceans most elusive creatures, the dugong.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The teams are at the Old College, a part of Aberystwyth University on the West Coast of Wales. A grade one listed building and the first University in Wales. Our experts Christina Trevanion and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Christina values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj. Paul Martin visits a Victorian tourist attraction that paved the way for modern photography.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Michelle Ackerley, Katie Derham, Karen Hauer and Geoff Norcott test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Redemption Week.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The week reaches its finale as Vernon Kay hosts the last stop on this weeks UK-wide adventure. TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz have one final shot at glory, with Paul Gorton leading an unreliable tour of Coleraine.

programme.title: The Wild Gardener
programme.desc: Colin digs ponds, clears brambles, plants woodland and sews meadows as he tries to figure out how best to lure the wild creatures back. Along the way, Colin travels across Britain and Ireland to meet other enthusiasts on their own wild crusades.

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty looks back at some of the remarkable gardeners shining a light on how people can grow their own nutritious food fresh from the garden.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The team returns to Okavango Delta to catch up with old friends, lionesses hunt nonstop to feed growing cubs, leopardess Xudum moves on, and the new mum, Lediba, steps into the spotlight. Pobe the cheetah returns with two cubs of her own.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The magnificent Kae Kurd introduces comedians Laura Smyth and Liam Farrelly to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the days top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Scrapper
programme.desc: Dreamy 12-year-old Georgie lives happily in her London flat, filling it with magic. When her estranged father turns up, he forces her to confront reality.

programme.title: Relic
programme.desc: When family matriarch Edna goes missing, her daughter and granddaughter return home to find her. They discover a haunting presence hanging over the home, which is taking over Ednas mind.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: The Rising Cost of Health Benefits
programme.desc: Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates the spiralling costs of the health-related benefits system and asks whether it is fit for purpose.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent north west England. Casting director Debbie McWilliams helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.desc: The hunters venture into the Everglades Triangle, an area known for mysterious disappearances and swarms of snakes; Troy, Chase and Bill use a drone to search the swamp from the air, while Bruce and Zak track snakes with a handheld thermal device.

programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team travel to Wood Green to help members of the public do battle with antiques dealers Mark Stevens, Ian Towning, Jan Keyne and David Ford. Today, a gold pocket watch and Albert chain causes the most excitement.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Barry utilises a little-known tactic to snatch up a locker, and it ties him up in knots; Brandi shops in the bargain basement, but puts on quite a show in the process; Ivy lands a large unit and the tools stack right up.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: On Nov 22, 1963, President John F Kennedy is assassinated, shaking the nation to its core. While an initial suspect is captured, many believe theres more to the story. Investigators and amateur sleuths alike have been searching for answers.

programme.title: Tish Tash
programme.desc: The family are going out for pizza. Tish is very excited and knows exactly what shell order. Shes disappointed to find the restaurant is closed. Rosa and Pola suggest a Mexican place, but Tish doesnt know what Mexican food is like.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing goes to play with Sula, who is engrossed in a game of sparkle magic when he arrives. Bing wants to play too but Sula wont let him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies eat lots of Tubby Toast and make a huge pile of crumbs. Luckily, the Noo-Noo is on hand to clean up.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee finds that its easier to blame Action Ape for all of his mess, but then feels guilty when Mo tells Action Ape off. Can Tee learn to tell Mo the truth?

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea wears a giant powersuit so powerful it knocks Supertato across the supermarket aisles. Carrot and Supertato plan to outsmart Evil Pea so shell lose her new powers.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Amma surprise Bing to show him Sulas sparkly painted butterfly face. Bing also wants a painted face, one that looks like Biteysaurus, and Sula says she can do it for him. Sula begins smothering his face in Biteys signature green, but when she accidentally adds purple spots, Bing is horrified! He wants Sula to go home. But then Flop adds some teeth to Bings Biteysaurus, so he can really be Bitey.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: A camping trip with Preston goes very wrong when he wont let them use Mojos tops. But camping Prestons way soon starts a fire - its Mojo and Bo to the rescue!

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Introducing you to Squirrel Club are Happys Mum and Norries Dad. There is a lot to see, starting with Duggee walking along, but hes in two halves. Next is an instalment of the Squirrels favourite comic book, Detective Puppy.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is having fun looking at nature in the garden. When Roly wants to join in, she tells him he needs to be really quiet, but he gets overexcited, shouting so loudly that he scares a lovely butterfly away. Norrie is put out because Roly did the opposite of what she said. When Roly asks what an opposite is, Norrie struggles to explain. Luckily, Duggee has his Opposites Badge and helps the squirrels understand by using lots of fun examples.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want to play restaurant with Grandad, but its a little different than hes used to. Hes going to need an app to order the food.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad has rudely plonked himself on one end of the seesaw. So Bluey and Bingo round up the entire playground to help flip him off, but they still need to find one more friend.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitchs hopes of winning a solar-powered race across the Australian outback are foiled when he crashes the Glitchster in the middle of the night. With the solar battery dead, he and Kyan are stranded with no water and no way out. Its up to the Go Jetters to find them and save the day.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Scare Owl
programme.desc: When Peter uses a model owl to scare off Old Brown, he ends up scaring the squirrels too!

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks Space Division finally solve the mystery of the space signal.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: From A to Z, every Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. When the Alphablocks cant decide between rock and pop, its time for a battle of the bands to see which is best.

programme.title: Tiny Wonders
programme.desc: Its time for a picnic. The Noggle is landing, so there must be a Tiny Wonder to explore. The Nogglins discover something crunchy, and Fidd slips and slides into trouble.

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: My UltraTurbo Challenge will make everything else seem slow! Reach terminal velocity with fast feet, climbers and star jumps, then crank up the turbo for the Activate. Get ready to move with Joe and the Activators.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift visit the Cloud Kitchen, where Patch needs some help from the Playtimers to make hoopy owl bagels. Its time for a tasty adventure!

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the fastest woodwind player pear attempting to be a one-pear orchestra and play three woodwind instruments.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula have a lovely time in the park clambering around on the trees. At first it is Sula who needs Bings help to climb up, but when Bing climbs a bit too high he gets stuck and then he needs some help himself.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `pizza, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of pizza, a round pizza, a tomato pizza, a party pizza and a cheesy pizza! Theres also a banana pizza, and Dee plays a game of ping pong with a pizza.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Barnaby gets scared of the bigger horseflies but gets comfortable around a baby one and makes friends with it.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Brian asks the Piggies to help with his Giggle Sprouts party. Everyone thinks opening the pot will be easy, but it is actually impossible!

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer. Chickedy and Chick run along the rod to the very end. Toodloo runs along the rod and jumps to join Chickedy and Chick, causing the rod to spring up sending them flying.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin visits the vet with his friends pet dog for a check-up. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble pretends to be a vet for his toy patients.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winkys bag fills up with sparkles from a tap, and when Home Dome overflows with tubby custard, Po has to turn the Tubby Custard Machine off. A child in Tummy Tales uses a new tap in the bathroom when she brushes her teeth before bedtime.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Pip, Theo, Lola, Oakley, Tommy and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside Nigels bag are a duck sponge and stacking toy boats, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a fire station with her friend Abigail and plays a game of I Spy.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie and Triggs are helping Dad in the garden. When he asks them to fill the catering can, they get distracted by the strange echoes inside and are magically transported to the inside the belly of a whale.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Dylan imagines hes a hair stylist wants to give his client a new hairstyle theyll love, but getting it just right is a harder than he thought.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri attends an underwater party and finds she doesnt enjoy lake weed snacks.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: When Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes yummy drops get snatched by a group of Beetrootsaurus, so begins a game of pea-in-the-middle to get them back.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Blueys having fun copying Dad, but her game takes a turn when Bluey finds a hurt budgie.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is putting up a picture of himself and the old friends he grew up with, who are just like family to him. This inspires the Squirrels to take a photo of their own, so they take turns getting smartened up for the camera with varying degrees of success. After a number of experimental poses, they eventually settle on the perfect composition, until Tag spots Enid - shes part of the family too, so shouldnt she be in the photo?

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Everyone is having fun in the paddling pool, until Stripey pulls the stopper out of the valve. Disappointed, Timmy and Yabba try to find alternative pools.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we join sisters Eden and Ava as they become superheroes! First they create their characters Nature Bunny Girl and H-Winter Girl - not forgetting new personas for Mum and Dad too!

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Banoffi pie was thought to have been invented in Sussex, and today Sonny-Mac is showing us how to make it. Sonny-Mac puts bananas and cream on top of a tasty base of crushed biscuits. He also adds his special ingredient, dates, to make it lovely and sweet. Not all his friends like the taste of dates at first, so lets hope they enjoy them when the pie is ready. Sonny-Mac serves the pie to his friends at a special tea party, where everyone dresses very smartly.

programme.title: Ferne and Rorys Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Jasiri finds out some fascinating frog facts. Rory and his son Sandy hop like frogs to the local lochside and make a pond in their garden. Ferne visits an aquarium to meet some amazing amphibians, including poison dart frogs.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Charli and her disability assistance dog Tinks visit a Hobby Fair at Woofton Town Hall, where Charli is selling all the clever things she makes.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Mandrill and Sand Lizard
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet some colourful characters - the amazing mandrills, which are the largest species of monkey in the world. They get close enough to see the beautiful blue and red faces of the males. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet sand lizards. They are surrounded by many females, who blend in perfectly with their sandy home, as well as some colourful males, who are much easier to spot.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is banned from going anywhere near Sommersby School, so Gramps is at the Brooklyn-Bells house to cheer him up. They play a game together on the front drive. Gramps and Waffle run in circles quickly, then slowly, before falling to the ground with laughter. Mrs Hobbs appears at her front door. She might have known it would be Gramps and Waffle making the racket. Mrs Hobbs reveals shes doggy day-caring Connie and Rubys dog, Jelly. Gramps and Waffle cant believe it.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Flynne helps Oona to find Mossy, and proves she can be a friendly fox.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated preschool series that features four adorable potatoes who sing and dance as they explore.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Its been snowing in the garden, and Yukee helps Sonny find uses for a large collection of recycled bells.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are rushing around, so Duggee shows them how to enjoy things at a slower pace. However, they soon come across Tortoise, who needs to get to the hospital as his wife is having babies. Duggee and the Squirrels need to get him there fast!

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say boots. See lots of different boots and explore how they look and feel. Discover blue boots, new boots, welly boots and even smelly boots! There are boots to wear in a puddle or when you cuddle.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Ten launches her rocket Rides into space using a rekenrek, a counting frame with ten beads on each row, which helps calculate the value of each ride into pennies.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: TH, I and NG make something spectacular - but what is it? Its marvellous, mechanical and musical and everyone wants one! This episode introduces the th- digraph and the words thing, this, that, thick, thin and them.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin and his friend go to the library and finish their day with a treat in the library cafe. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble uses his imagination by playing library with the toys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: After a fun waving game with the Tiddlytubbies, the Teletubbies enjoy watching children waving as they set off on a short boat trip. Finally, the Teletubbies wave to the Tiddlytubbies, who are off to sleepybyes.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Penguin has a problem - he wants to run fast, but he doesnt know how! Will the Patchwork Pals find a way to help him?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.sub-title: Raa Raa and the Jungle Lookout
programme.desc: Raa Raa and his friends launch Hufftys whooshy whooshers into the air and lose them in the jungle, but teaming up to look for them can be just as fun as launching them.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Wilson and Brewster get caught in the cold.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Hopefully Arthur Sleep will wake up in time to introduce all the fun to be had. Things dont go quite to plan when clumsy antiques expert Ann Teak lends a hand to a vase restorer.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace explains the prominent features of three models of motorbike and demonstrates how to ride them.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts use magnets to try to stop a meteor thats heading toward the Octopod and get an assist from a rock-chomping humphead parrotfish and her friends.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Unguarded Garden
programme.desc: When Mr McGregor goes away for the day, Peter cant wait to raid the garden. However, will the absence of one foe leave Peter and his little sister at the mercy of another?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Its time for the annual animal obstacle course race, and the animals need to work together in teams to complete the course. Duggee has his obstacle course badge. He and the Squirrels form the RED team and the fun begins.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Vida helps Sunny taste new things at the Sweetwood Food Festival.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato and Captain Pak Choi team up to get her pirate ship back from Evil Pea.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Its hard enough for Mojo and Bo to help Jackson get all his sheep back in their pen. But its even harder when the hungry sheep start chewing up Prestons flower festival!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want to play restaurant with Grandad, but its a little different than hes used to. Hes going to need an app to order the food.

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafis tall wish shows that growing up too fast can mean missing the joy of being little.

programme.desc: Great Gran Gran shows JoJo her St Lucian local newsletter, and JoJo is inspired to make her own community newsletter. They go out and about finding news for it. Cynthia tells them that a mystery guest is turning on the winter market lights, Charlene shares that she has special bunting for sale at the winter market, and Navid tells them about his new food stall. JoJo and Gran Gran get working on making their newsletter.

programme.desc: Lee Israel is a frustrated, hard-drinking author who can barely afford to pay her rent or bills in 1990s New York. When the dealers start to catch on, Lee recruits a dubious friend to help her continue her self-destructive cycle of trickery and deceit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Monkey Gets Away
programme.desc: Rachel accidentally lets Ross pet monkey escape, and the friends form a search party. She is then horrified to learn that her former best friend is engaged to marry her ex-fiance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Evil Orthodontist
programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiance Barry, even though hes engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fake Monica
programme.desc: Someone is pretending to be Monica, using her credit card numbers and having the sort of fun that Monica wishes she could have.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ick Factor
programme.desc: Monica discovers that shes developed a highly mature approach to her latest love affair, and Ross finds out that hes being mistaken for a male prostitute.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Carol gives birth, Ross and Susan almost miss the event when Phoebe accidentally locks them in a cupboard together to put a stop to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Joey befriends an unmarried pregnant woman and ends up helping her to give birth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross has been in love with Rachel since ninth grade but has always kept it a secret. Now the truth accidentally comes out, but as Rachel rushes to the airport to greet Ross on his return from a business trip, she discovers that work is not all hes been getting up to while hes been away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Ross comes back from China with his new girlfriend Julie, and Rachel has a really hard time dealing with it. She winds up sleeping with Paolo and hates herself for it. Joey persuades her to tell Ross how she feels, but when Ross tells her he is in love with Julie, she cant bring herself to do it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Breast Milk
programme.desc: While Carol and Susan are away, Ross and the friends babysit his newborn son Ben, but Ross is disgusted when Phoebe tests the temperature of some bottled breast milk on her wrist. Meanwhile, Joey competes with a fellow cologne salesman.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Worrying that Ray only visits her to get a good meal, Marie hesitates to teach Debra her cooking secrets.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond takes over the family finances to prove to Debra how easy it is, but he soon discovers that hes no accountant.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond gains new respect for his brother when he sees him risk his life on the job.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra cant get a good nights sleep when Ally fears there are monsters in her room.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra discovers that her mother-in-law likes Roberts girlfriend better than she likes her because the girlfriend is a `good girl. Jealous of the attention, Raymond minimises Debras premarital activities as well.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gets into a fight with another parent who accuses her of bringing an unhealthy snack to a T-ball game.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert experiments with a ventriloquists dummy to make his driving school lectures more interesting.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray becomes concerned with his own mortality when he discovers that he has shrunk a quarter of an inch and is no longer six feet tall, and Marie is mad with Frank for selling half of their burial plot without asking her.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is shocked when Debra takes all their baby clothes to a garage sale. He didnt know theyd decided not to have any more children, and their disagreement soon becomes a family affair when Marie starts dishing out unwanted advice.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray makes the mistake of asking Debra why she married him, rekindling memories of his proposal and his familys involvement, and bringing back Rays fears that Debra felt pressured into saying yes.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is afraid that Debra is marrying him for the wrong reason.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray moves his family into his parents house when the termite exterminators visit, and Debra takes the opportunity to turn the tables by interfering in Frank and Maries life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Frank backs into Roberts police car, Debra decides that hes not a safe driver and that the kids cant travel in his car.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra hires a babysitter but becomes jealous of her popularity with the kids, and her plans to remedy the situation end in disaster.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray embarrasses Debra at a school auction, and worries about his wife taking revenge on him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is shocked to realise she wishes her mum was more like Marie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When downstairs neighbour Mr Heckles dies, the friends clean out his apartment, but Chandler becomes convinced his hyper-critical nature will mean he ends up alone and sad, just like Heckles.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is surprised when Phoebes husband - a gay Canadian ice dancer - shows up, as nobody knew she was married. Elsewhere, Chandler reveals his third nipple, and Ross new relationship is under attack, unbeknown to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant
programme.desc: Monica gets promoted and invites the friends out for dinner to celebrate, but problems arise when the difference in their salaries becomes apparent and the group splits in two. Meanwhile, when Chandler sets a woman up to get a date with her, a case of mistaken identities means that he discovers what she really thought of him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachels negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds out that her picture of her father, whom she has never known, is just a generic photograph that comes with the picture frame. Elsewhere, after Ross makes Monicas heater malfunction, her Christmas party guests swelter in the middle of winter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gets a great acting opportunity on `Days of our Lives, Rachel dates a guy called Russ who is amazingly similar to Ross, and Monica renews her relationship with `Fun Bobby, but finds out Bobby is only fun when he is drinking.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Lesbian Wedding
programme.desc: Rachels free-spirited mother visits and reveals that she wants to end her marriage and experiment sexually. Meanwhile, Carol and Susan prepare for their wedding, with some help from an uncomfortable Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Joey starts dating a fan who is gorgeous, but soon discovers that shes also quite mad. Meanwhile, Ross is reminded how much he misses Marcel his old pet monkey, and travels to San Diego Zoo to find out what has happened to him.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: When Nick inherits some money from his late father, Jess is determined to put the funds to good use by secretly tackling Nicks box of unwanted responsibilities - and his unpaid bills.

programme.title: New Girl

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: South Park

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Ross finally spends some time with Marcel after many false starts, and Chandler is delighted to get a date with the sexy Susie, who he hasnt seen since fourth grade. However, she gets her revenge for youthful humiliation by leaving him in the loo, naked but for a pair of her knickers.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Prom Video
programme.desc: When Monicas parents clear out her old room to install a gym, they unearth an old video of her and Rachels first prom night. Its obvious from this that Ross is in love with Rachel and, having told him it will never happen, Rachel changes her mind. Elsewhere, Joey gives Chandler a hideous bracelet as a token of his friendship and is mortally offended to hear Chandler saying how terrible it is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel ... You Know
programme.desc: Among the guests at Monicas party is a handsome and sophisticated doctor, but the only problem is that hes old enough to be her father. Meanwhile, Joey buys a big screen TV and two reclining leather armchairs with the money from his `Days of Our Lives contract.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Moves Out
programme.desc: Everyone at Monicas dads birthday party finds it amusing that their middle-aged friend, eye-doctor Richard Burke, is dating a woman in her 20s - until Monicas parents learn that it is their daughter. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey row over a dirty spoon. Is the apartment still big enough for both of them?

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Moves In
programme.desc: With Joey in his new apartment, Chandler gets a troublesome new flatmate, Eddie. Meanwhile, Phoebe makes a video for `Smelly Cat, and Ross and Monica find it nearly impossible to live together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Dr Ramoray Dies
programme.desc: Joeys remarks to a fan magazine could cause him to lose his job playing Dr Ramoray on a daytime television drama, and Chandlers new flatmate Eddie becomes too annoying to cope with.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Wont Go
programme.desc: Chandler wants his annoying new roommate to move out, Joey is forced to face some exorbitant bills, and the girls learn from a book about personal empowerment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Old Yeller Dies
programme.desc: Monicas new boyfriend impresses Chandler and Joey, Phoebe finds out what she missed as a child, and Rachel resents the fact that Ross is planning the rest of their lives together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is broke and decides to gamble on the stock market, and Chandler and Ross run into a couple of bullies in Central Park. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey help Phoebe locate her father, but a vicious dog prevents Phoebe from finally meeting him. After accidentally injuring the dog with her car, Phoebe gets to know her fathers second wife whom she never knew about, and another long-lost family member.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides to hold a surprise birthday for Rachel, but everything goes wrong when Rachels estranged parents turn up unexpectedly. The party then splits into two factions: a dreary gathering in Monicas apartment, and a college-style drunken party at Chandler and Joeys flat.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Chicken Pox
programme.desc: Phoebes submariner boyfriend visits New York on leave from the Navy. Unfortunately, Phoebe catches chicken pox at baby Bens first birthday party. Meanwhile, when Monica realises that Richard has no real neuroses, she worries that she is too strange for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Barry and Mindys Wedding
programme.desc: When Rachel serves as a bridesmaid at the wedding of her ex-fiance Barry and her friend Mindy, she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her.

programme.desc: For this tour Mr Evans embarked on a sixty night trek around the UK, performing for more fans than ever before! Expect more of his non-stop, manic and energetic comedy routines.

programme.desc: Monicas new boyfriend impresses Chandler and Joey, Phoebe finds out what she missed as a child, and Rachel resents the fact that Ross is planning the rest of their lives together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is broke and decides to gamble on the stock market, and Chandler and Ross run into a couple of bullies in Central Park. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey help Phoebe locate her father, but a vicious dog prevents Phoebe from finally meeting him. After accidentally injuring the dog with her car, Phoebe gets to know her fathers second wife whom she never knew about, and another long-lost family member.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides to hold a surprise birthday for Rachel, but everything goes wrong when Rachels estranged parents turn up unexpectedly. The party then splits into two factions: a dreary gathering in Monicas apartment, and a college-style drunken party at Chandler and Joeys flat.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Chicken Pox
programme.desc: Phoebes submariner boyfriend visits New York on leave from the Navy. Unfortunately, Phoebe catches chicken pox at baby Bens first birthday party. Meanwhile, when Monica realises that Richard has no real neuroses, she worries that she is too strange for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Barry and Mindys Wedding
programme.desc: When Rachel serves as a bridesmaid at the wedding of her ex-fiance Barry and her friend Mindy, she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her.

programme.title: Lee Evans: Roadrunner - Live at the O2
programme.desc: For this tour Mr Evans embarked on a sixty night trek around the UK, performing for more fans than ever before! Expect more of his non-stop, manic and energetic comedy routines.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Brand New Tour
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges returns to the state to give his take on social media, anxiety, conversations with his dog, and having Barack Obama as his support act.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Actress Lyndia Greenwood and guest comedian Brad Sherwood join the regulars for a series of hilarious games, including Hollywood Director, Duet, Film Dub, Dbbing, Living Scenery, and Scenes from a Hat.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts this skit comedy show where the actors on the show, usually Wayne Brady, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles and another guest star or two do different comedy skits. Its all improv and made up on the spot.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers
programme.desc: The Dieselsellerz crew race to finish their monster mud truck, and The Muscle rushes to get a buyer for the Denali he bought without Heavy Ds permission.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: Two rookie miners in MT desperately need to double their gold after a disastrous start to the season.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: With nearly forty years of mining under his belt, Freddy Dodge goes on the road to help struggling mine owners to hit the mother lode. He teams up with Juan Ibarra to overhaul washplants, introduce gold recovery techniques and transform operations.

programme.title: Overhaulin
programme.desc: An accident survivors dream of owning a Mustang comes true.

programme.title: Overhaulin
programme.desc: A woman, dealing with a serious illness, would be overjoyed if her Mustang were overhauled.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: Keith and Jake deploy guerrilla tactics when a factory trawler invades their grounds. At the Russian Line, Rick encounters temperatures that even snow crab cant survive on deck, and Sig struggles against mounting breakdowns threatening his delivery.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: After six relentless weeks at sea, man and machine push past the breaking point. While Keith seeks medical care in Anchorage, the Wizard loses steering in 20-foot seas.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.desc: Find out how individual transporters, post clocks, drum heads and drums are made, as more everyday items are put under the microscope.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.sub-title: Water Heaters, Air Bags, Jelly Beans
programme.desc: Find out how residential water heaters, air bags, jelly beans and ice resurfacers are made, as more everyday items are put under the microscope.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.sub-title: Amphibious Vehicles/ Putters/ Model Boats/ Coloured Crayons
programme.desc: Find out how amphibious vehicles, putters, model ships, and coloured crayons are made.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.desc: Footballs, guitar amplifiers, marbles and aeroplane propellers: more everyday items are put under the microscope to find out how they are made.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys are faced with the daunting task of overhauling a faulty electrical system and building the first hydro-electric wheel on the Little Sustina River.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys attempt to save a single mother and her daughter from a rampant moose, Marty builds a bridge across a creek and Matt teaches predator deterrent techniques.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds a rare, rally-bred 1972 Lancia Fulvia. He and Ant must fix its bad CV joints, a broken heater, cracked dash and a debris-clogged fuel system.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Expedition Files
programme.desc: High on Everests icy slopes, two climbers vanish into legend. In a quiet West Virginia town, a ghost demands justice from beyond the grave. And learn the real story of Mata Hari -- the exotic dancer condemned as a German spy.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh dives the deadly, frigid waters of the Baltic Sea in search of a revolutionary Nazi submarine: the V-80. Hitler ordered it scuttled to prevent the Allies from learning its secrets. Now, Josh risks it all to bring the prototype to the surface.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh braves the African savanna to investigate the rampage of two lions who killed as many as 135 railroad workers in 1898. To prevent more bloodshed, Josh joins wildlife rangers tracking a pair of rogue lions who are on a modern-day killing spree.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.desc: Plastic bottles, eggs, jars and handcrafted wooden pens: more everyday objects are put under the microscope to find out how they were made.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Declassified video captured by the U.S. Navy triggers Jess and Phils investigation into mysterious, supersonic craft flying above -- and racing below -- the waters off Californias Catalina Island.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Jess and Phils investigation into the phenomenon of unidentified submerged objects off the coast of Catalina Island intensifies after the discovery of an extraterrestrial underwater base between the island and the mainland.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Josh Gates dives into one of Americas oldest witch stories. In the Tennessee backwoods, locals report being attacked in a vast cave system. Jess and Phil go underground and uncover frightening piles of bones as well as potential human remains.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Hikers are going missing amid reports of grotesque, human-like creatures prowling Appalachias Great Smoky Mountains; Phil and Jess venture into the unforgiving wilderness to investigate the truth behind terrifying sightings of feral people.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Horrified locals in southern Iowa are reporting sightings of a mysterious beast that terrorised the town of Van Meter 100 years ago; Phil and Jess are on a mission to unearth clues to whats lurking in the shadows.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.sub-title: Ghost Fort of the Civil War

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh dives the deadly, frigid waters of the Baltic Sea in search of a revolutionary Nazi submarine: the V-80. Hitler ordered it scuttled to prevent the Allies from learning its secrets. Now, Josh risks it all to bring the prototype to the surface.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh braves the African savanna to investigate the rampage of two lions who killed as many as 135 railroad workers in 1898. To prevent more bloodshed, Josh joins wildlife rangers tracking a pair of rogue lions who are on a modern-day killing spree.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: Gordon heads to America in a bid to rescue failing restaurant owners from imminent disaster and provide them with a recipe for success. In this first episode, Gordon visits the Hot Potato Cafe in Fishtown, Philadelphia. With no profit made in its two years of trading and an influential restaurant reviewer dubbing them `Spuddy Hell, Gordon soon realises Hot Potato may have had its chips.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Flamangos in rural Whitehouse, New Jersey. Mother and daughter owners Adele and Cheryl are stuck in their tropical ways but their restaurant is far from paradise. They are in deep financial trouble and Adele struggles to get on with her staff, is disappointed by her chef and hates Gordon with a passion. Gordon has one last chance to bring them all around and make the restaurant a success.

programme.title: The Kid Detective
programme.desc: A once-celebrated kid detective, now 31, continues to solve the same trivial mysteries between hangovers and bouts of self-pity. Until a naive client brings him his first `adult case, to find out who brutally murdered her boyfriend.

programme.desc: After a stint in prison, a diamond thief has high hopes of going straight, until he is offered a job he cant refuse.

programme.title: Patton
programme.desc: Biopic of the US general who successfully fought the Germans in the latter days of World War II, George S Patton, but who was frequently in conflict with his superiors.

programme.title: Ride Lonesome
programme.desc: A sheriff-turned-bounty hunter captures a notorious gunfighter, hoping to flush out his murderous brother and provoke a bloody shoot-out, but he finds several others are following the same prey.

programme.title: Twelve OClock High
programme.desc: "Were in a war, a shooting war. Weve got to fight, and some of us have got to die". Frank Savage is the WWII commander drawing every ounce of fight from his weary airmen.

programme.title: Mrs Doubtfire
programme.desc: Slapstick film about a divorced actor who is willing to go to any lengths to get his children back, even posing as a matronly Scottish housekeeper. His family, taken in by the disguise, decide to employ the eccentric but apparently capable widow, and everyone is happy with the arrangement - until the mothers old boyfriend reappears and tries to become a father figure to the children.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernseys wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Northern Exposure
programme.desc: The Bikers fall immediately in love with the beautiful and tranquil lakes of Finland and enjoy what they think will be a completely different change of pace from the intensity of St Petersburg. Dave learns how to have a traditional Finnish sauna experience while Si makes himself feel right at home cooking up Finnish pastries.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree packs a kitchen-equipment care package for her daughter; theres a Quarter Sheet Pan Supper thats fast to make after a day in the office; Wonton Taco Cups made in a mini-cupcake tin; a nonstick skillet to cook a Cheddar and Apple Grilled Cheese.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes 16-minute Italian meals; theres speedy pork scaloppini for a quick family dinner and snappy steak pizzaiola that the boys absolutely love.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Butter Chicken Curry, Pesto-Glazed Salmon En Croûte
programme.desc: Jamie shares his take on butter chicken curry, featuring less spice, fewer ingredients and less washing-up, and a pesto-glazed salmon en croûte on a bed of greens.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver rustles up hoisin-glazed pork with a tear-and-share steamed bun, makes a veggie toad in the hole with mushrooms, and enjoys a steak date night with Jools.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Nothing turns up the volume more in cooking than herbs. Ina is sharing her top herbs and how to use them in Greek lamb with a yogurt mint sauce and oregano, feta and tomato salad.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is writing an article about eating local and shes challenged herself to make a meal entirely from Hamptons ingredients, including an eggplant caponata with produce from a farm in Water Mill.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Ten passionate homebakers take part in a `bake off, which will test every aspect of their baking skills as they battle it out to be crowned the Great British Bake Offs Best Amateur Baker. The bakers tackle three increasingly difficult challenges, starting off with their signature bake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins take the eight passionate home bakers who made it through the first round to Scone Palace near Perth to make biscuits and teatime treats.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Caseys mouthwatering mission brings him to Tennessee, where he faces his hottest challenge yet, three jumbo chicken tenders smothered in a Carolina Reaper-infused sauce.

programme.title: Chef Grudge Match
programme.desc: Laila Ali welcomes four culinary bros to duke it out in the kitchen. With the help of chefs Maneet Chauhan and Stephanie Izard, Johnny Bananas calls out fellow Worst Cooks in America competitor Mark Long. Michael Hung settles a beef with Chris Oh.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri travels the country to dive into some meaty masterpieces, including a poutine burger in Colorado, potato chip pie in Dallas, and killer clay-pot chicken in Phoenix.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri crosses the Atlantic to dive into the many different cuisines of Barcelona, Spain. Here he samples Galician-style octopus and soup, traditional sausage and old-school Catalan cooking.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri travels to Barcelona, Spain, and digs into everything from top-notch tapas, like prawns and quail, to traditional Catalan specialties like mouthwatering meatballs and even a veal-head stew.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy unhinges his jaw for some mega-mouthful meals. In Louisville, a funky seafood spot piles their specialties on top of waffles, and in Portland, a killer barbecue food truck stacks a fully loaded `shabamwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. Adam heads to Port Royal Grille in New York state and attempts the Davy Jones Locker Challenge.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. Adam takes on the Fried Catfish Challenge, attempting to eat 29 catfish fillets.

programme.desc: Buddy is asked to make wedding cakes for fourteen couples getting married in a mass wedding on Valentines Day, and cousin Anthony asks someone at the bakery to be his valentine.

programme.title: Cake Boss

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri hits the road for an off-the-chain culinary journey. On the big island, a Filipino-Hawaiian mash-up is famous for leaving diners speechless. In Chicago, a Spanish-style spot serves seafood that might just surprise you.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieris going from coast to coast to dig up dynamite dishes. In Los Angeles, he checks out a Mexican joint in Grand Central Market serving outrageous octopus tostadas and putting their spin on a burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris grabbing standout sandwiches, including a primo pork burger in Longmont, Colorado, and diving into some serious spice with fiery Thai specialities in Los Angeles.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina reveals the secrets of cooking with wine and gets the low down on cheese and wine pairings from her friend and wine expert Martine Warner.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina gets a tagine cooking lesson from teacher and private chef Gail Arnold, then shares her beginners recipe that will get even amateur chefs cooking.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina creates the perfect French dinner party, with slow cooked lamb and tips on how to create the ideal French dinner party setting.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Former foodstore owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina goes back to basics and cooks sweet and savoury rice dishes, from a jambalaya master class to the classic rice pudding with the volume turned up.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina takes on egg recipes with an omelette master class, then reveals the secrets to perfect souffle and gives the scoop on foolproof hollandaise.

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain turns her attention to an ingredient that many people find irresistible: chocolate. Nadiya shares four recipes of pure, unadulterated, chocolatey bliss. First up, is Nadiyas ultimate Money Cant Buy You Happiness Brownie.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: On this leg of his trip, James is in Bresse, home of one Frances most famous ingredients - the Bresse chicken and uses the revered fowl in his splendid chicken blanquette.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James is in Burgundy, a region famous for its wines and mustard fields. Re-tracing Keith Floyds visit 30 years ago, James stops at the same vineyard for a personal tour.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary heads to a festival where food takes centre stage. Set in the grounds of Cornbury Park in Oxfordshire, performers from theatre, music and art come together, but the real headliners are the top chefs who host sharing feasts and intimate dinners.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary heads to Edinburgh. Her late mother was Scottish, so it holds a very special place in her heart. The city of Edinburgh is full of excitement as it gears up for the marathon.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Everyday
programme.desc: Mary travels to Scotland to enjoy the best of their everyday larder with recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, and shares the joy of venison cooked on a BBQ with chef Tom Kitchin.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary shares inspiring everyday dishes full of wholesome goodness from her secret tip for a rich bolognaise to a heart-warming soup. She dons a white suit and tries her hand at bee keeping, collecting fresh honey to make a winning pud.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary opens her garden to host a village fete and shares her brilliant flair for turning easy dishes into spectacular crowd pleasers. Shes visited by two passionate brothers for a lesson in the in the secrets of authentic Italian pizza making.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: This is indulgence at its best with Marys mouth-watering dishes that give the everyday a lift with lashings of naughtiness. Treats are in store as Mary pursues the history of the Great British pie and tempts us with the secret to an easy sticky toffee pud.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary shares some inspirational ideas to make everyday family meals exciting with fabulous no fuss dishes. She tries her hand at milking a goat and shows her granddaughter how to make her own goats cheese for a family picnic.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.sub-title: When You Want to Show Off
programme.desc: As Mary and her husband reach their 50th wedding anniversary its time to push the boat out. Mary shares her secrets to surprisingly easy showstoppers, recreates canapes from the 1960s and finishes the series with sparkle.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam finds secret eats in Warsaw, Poland, including a Polish spin on Neapolitan pizza, an off-menu version of a classic Polish street food known as a zapiekanka, and a home cooking restaurant hidden inside an apartment building.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam travels to Rome to uncover the citys culinary secrets, including a unique take on Cacio e Pepe at a restaurant located in a castle-walled city, traditional porchetta in limited supply and an off-menu pasta dish.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.
